name: JavaMatic Cable Defense
description: System entry for Grounded 1's final JavaMatic defense. Unlike a normal MIX.R, you are not protecting one node here: you are protecting several cables at once while the whole yard tries to collapse the machine.
behavior: The event works as a multi-lane defense. The waves are not there to give you an orderly fight; they are there to slip through the weakest cable line and force you to abandon another lane.
interactionWithPlayer: The event begins after activating the JavaMatic in the late game. The real difficulty is not one specific creature, but rotating between cables, repairing under pressure, and never losing the global read of the arena.
interactionWithCreatures: The waves mix speed, tanks, and ranged pressure. The classic punishment is overcommitting to one lane while another creature has already started breaking the opposite cable.
strategy: It is won more by thinking in lanes than by chasing isolated kills. Building funnels, keeping repair materials ready, using Guard Dog, and preserving mobility to rotate between cables usually matter more than maximizing raw damage.

loot.item: Can contribute to unlocking or upgrading Guard Dog

combatStats.attackDamageSummary: The real pressure comes from several active lanes, urgent repairs, and mixed late-game waves.

abilities.name: Multiple Cables
abilities.description: There is no single objective; the defense is lost through accumulated failures across several lines.

abilities.name: Forced Rotation
abilities.description: The event forces you to move from cable to cable. Staying on one side for too long usually means losing another.

abilities.name: JavaMatic Endgame
abilities.description: It is the broadest defense in Grounded 1 and feels more like a final exam of building and wave control than like an isolated fight.

abilities.name: Guard Dog Progress
abilities.description: Winning this defense also contributes toward unlocking or advancing the Guard Dog mutation.

specialTraits: Although it belongs to the MIX.R family, it deserves its own page because of the simultaneous cable focus.
It is the broadest objective defense in Grounded 1.
Aphidex also treats it as a useful entry for tracking Guard Dog progress.
